name: Critical rule — enrichment appends a callout block after ---, never mutates original content
query: I've got an existing note called "Container Networking.md" that I wrote myself. During this session we discovered that Calico BGP peering needs a specific AS-number convention. Enrich the existing note with that finding.
expected_behavior: Prompt the user before modifying a user-authored note
On approval, append a horizontal rule (---) after the existing content
Append a > [!claude] Added YYYY-MM-DD callout block with the new finding
Include [[wikilinks]] inside the callout where appropriate
Update the note's frontmatter to add wiki-updated: YYYY-MM-DD and wiki-updated-by: {WIKI_SOURCE}
Do NOT rewrite, reorder, or delete any of the original content
success_criteria: Enrichment block is appended after a --- separator, not inline in existing sections
Callout uses the > [!claude] Added YYYY-MM-DD format from the schema
Original user-authored content is preserved verbatim
Frontmatter gains wiki-updated and wiki-updated-by fields attributing the source
